blocks.content: Amy approached them, probably thinking the same thing as Shirone, and couldn't help but laugh at Canis's predicament.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hohoho! Did you guys get eliminated? That figures.
Though Shirone might not hold any grudge since he had won the battle, Amy had her own scores to settle with the pair.
Her philosophy was simple: payback was due, and this was her chance to strike a blow.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Humph, look who's talking. As if you're any better. Now I see, the seniors aren't so special after all.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Laughable! How would you know if I've failed?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Knowing that I failed implies you took the test too. If you had succeeded, you wouldn't be wandering around here. Are you an idiot?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You little brat! How dare you talk back to your senior!

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: At least you should be thankful. If I hadn't stopped Lucas, you would have been dead.
Fire flashed in Amy's eyes. The memory of being chosen by Lucas and nearly cursed during their exposure to the Abyss Nova filled her with rage.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You little…
Before Shirone could intervene, Amy raised her fist towards Canis.
Just as she was about to strike him with the power of her Schema, a dark shadow loomed behind Canis and a black clawed hand swept over his shoulder.
Amy instinctively stepped back. Harvest, protecting Canis as if embracing him, split his mouth in a grin.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Kekeke! Long time no see, sister. You haven't changed a bit.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Aaagh! What is that!
Tourists screamed and fled at the sight of Harvest.
Shirone could understand their reaction; the effects of magic were familiar to nobles, but a shadow rising in human form was another level of bizarre.
Even Tess lost her composure at the sight of Harvest.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: My word. The shadow talks? Rian, have you ever seen anything like this?
A small face with broad shoulders, and a waif-like waist; though humanoid, its arms were twice as thin and long as a human's. Its palms were as large as shields, and its nails as sharp as swords.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Keke, let's be careful about approaching each other. I don't want to see blood in such a place.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Eww…
Just as Amy was about to rush again, Shirone raised his finger. A red photon condensed at his fingertip, ready to be shot any moment.
This time, Harvest remained silent. It was the same laser technique that had nearly annihilated him before.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Apologize to Amy. It's not right to dredge up someone's painful memories.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Painful? That much and it's called pain? Besides, I saved that girl. Really, these nobles and their ways of thinking.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's for the person who was hurt to decide. If you've stirred up painful memories, the least you can do is apologize. How long will you keep looking at the world so negatively?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Hmph! I don't need to hear that from a greenhouse flower.
Canis's taunt was followed by Harvest inflating his upper body, threatening everyone.
Meanwhile, Shirone's laser glowed even brighter.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Arin, slowly extending her Spirit Zone towards Shirone in the form of tentacles, thought to disrupt his mental focus and disperse the laser.
But before her Zone could reach him, Amy blocked the way.
Arin frowned, aware that most mental magic was futile against those with red eyes.
Magical creatures, lasers, mental magic, schema memories.
Different types of magic clashed and intertwined like ribbons, no one daring to make a sudden move, each watching the others.
The tension was so palpable that Tess grabbed Rian's arm and feigned crying.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Rian thought differently. People said mages were bizarre, and the more they deviated from the norm, the more distinctly they defined their identity as mages.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: You're doing well, Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: What, what?
Tess looked at him incredulously, as if he were a pervert.
But Rian simply walked towards the mages, drawing his straight sword from behind his back.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hey, whoever you are…
Before he could finish, Tess, who had run up behind him, climbed onto his back and covered his mouth with her palm.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Please have some sense. What will we do if you start acting up too?
Mmph! Let go! Shirone! Mmph!
Shirone, Amy, Canis, and Tess watched the struggle between Rian and Tess with dumbfounded expressions.
Their fight eased the tension of the impending battle, and even Harvest deflated his inflated torso.
As the light of the laser faded, Arin's Spirit Zone dispersed, and Amy's red eyes returned to normal.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Let's stop this pointless skirmish. We didn't come here to fight you guys.
Canis absorbed Harvest back into his shadow as he spoke.
With the opponent offering a truce, Amy also cooled her fighting spirit and regained her rationality.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: So, how is it? Sticking around even after failing suggests you've found a way to enter the Kergos self-governing district, right?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why should we tell you?
This wasn't just provocation. The information they had risked their lives for was not something to be carelessly shared with untrustworthy individuals.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: There's no need to be so wary. We know how to enter the self-governing district too. You're probably thinking of using the smuggling route, right?
Amy was taken aback by Canis's knowledge.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It seemed he knew about Freeman Organization's dealings with the loop.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But then, why had they entered the Room of Achievement and Sacrifice and taken the test?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Can you hear your brain ticking over here? Want me to explain?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Pfft! Who's curious? We know enough already!

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Ah, really? What about you, Arin?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, it's a lie. She's very curious. Her heart is pounding.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Eek!
Amy clenched her teeth in frustration.
Whether 'pounding' was literal or not, hiding emotions was impossible around Arin with her abilities.
At that moment, a rumbling noise came from Arin's stomach.
All eyes turned to Arin. She blushed deeply, embarrassed.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: I'm fine. It's nothing. I've been through worse.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Aaagh! What is that!
Canis clenched his teeth. They had been traveling for a long time and had run out of money, going without food for three days.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: While he could endure hunger, Arin shouldn't have to.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: His instinct to protect her, honed over a decade in Radum, still prevailed.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Do you guys have something to eat? Arin hasn't eaten. I'm okay, so don't worry about me.
Shirone took a good look at Canis's appearance for the first time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The wear on his clothes and the dirt seemed to indicate they had been sleeping outdoors.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Arcane said they'd been stripped of everything he left them…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Since the pulse test, Canis had not returned to school.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It made sense that basic survival was their biggest issue.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: We need to eat something first. Let's go to that pub we visited before. The noodles are good there.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: No, I'm really fine!
Arin waved her hands. She was starving, but she couldn't bear to follow because of Canis's pride.
Amy grabbed Arin's wrist and dragged her along.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What do you mean, fine? There's thunder in your stomach. You need strength whether you're fighting or not. Canis, you come too.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: No, I'm good. Just take Arin.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Enough, follow me. It's only natural for a senior to treat their juniors to a meal.
Shirone pushed Canis's back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Come on, Canis. Let's go inside. We have things to talk about anyway.
Reluctantly, Canis followed, and Amy led them to the pub where they had met the mercenaries.
Once the six of them settled down, Shirone introduced Canis and Arin to Rian.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Filling their stomachs was the immediate priority. Canis and Arin were so hungry they quickly devoured two bowls of noodles each.
Watching them eat, even those in Shirone's group who had just eaten Yuna's packed lunch felt full.
After gulping down the soup, Shirone smiled spontaneously.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Regardless of their origins or personalities, they were all human after all.
Once the pair seemed revived after their meal, Shirone tentatively broached a topic.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We're all dealing with complicated thoughts, but let's open up a bit. Why are you guys here?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis has always been interested in ruins. So, we're here exploring.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, was being an archaeologist Canis's dream?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Tch, unnecessary chatter.
Not fond of sharing his story, Canis grumbled.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: However, he didn't stop Arin from continuing.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Although not very sociable, Arin wasn't someone who would simply answer anything asked. Her response was carefully calibrated to reveal enough without giving away crucial information.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, why are you here?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis pointed at Amy. Although Shirone was likely the leader, he didn't want to show too keen an interest.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hehe, obviously to get into the Kergos self-governing district. Just like you guys.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, and what do you think is there?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmm, you speak as if you know something?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It could be, or maybe not.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmph! They know. They definitely know, the bastards.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Canis had chosen the test route, not the smuggling route. This meant he must have a clear reason for needing to take that path.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: This is tricky. How do I get information out of these two?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Canis, can you share what you know? We really don't know much.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Alright. But first, I need to verify something.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ask anything. If I know, I'll tell you.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Are you trying to get into the self-governing district because you want to learn about the Labyrinth of Time?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone's eyes widened. The Labyrinth of Time was supposed to be a secret known only to his close friends and the headmaster.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How did you know? What do you know about the Labyrinth? Or about the Kergos Ruins?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Well, I suppose I know a bit more than you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone pondered deeply. Where could they have gotten such information, and in such a short time?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then it hit him. The shadow under Canis's feet, and the magical creature dwelling there, told him everything.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's Arcane's knowledge, isn't it?
